evidence.quote: i defy anyone to claim with a straight face that this is clear and easy to follow: Autonomous-loop authorization (the `mandate-008` exception for the deterministic loop, binding).** `mandate-008` — a subagent returns an issue → STOP, report, the user decides — governs when the continue/stop signal is an LLM judgement (a blocker, an ambiguity, a scope/value/authority call). It does NOT stop the iterate-to-zero loop of steps 2→3→4. Autonomous re-dispatch of a FRESH agent (a fix agent, then a fresh adversarial re-audit) IS permitted and REQUIRED iff the loop terminates on a DETERMINISTIC gate — `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` exit 0 and the runtime-demo real-check, never an LLM read of "looks clean" — AND a SEPARATE fresh adversarial audit (not the implementer) reports every acceptance criterion CONFIRMED. `done ≡ (deterministic gate exit 0) AND (every success criterion CONFIRMED)` — even if the audit's total findings

**Autonomous-loop authorization (the `mandate-008` exception for the deterministic loop, binding).** `mandate-008` — a subagent returns an issue → STOP, report, the user decides — governs when the continue/stop signal is an LLM judgement (a blocker, an ambiguity, a scope/value/authority call). It does NOT stop the iterate-to-zero loop of steps 2→3→4. Autonomous re-dispatch of a FRESH agent (a fix agent, then a fresh adversarial re-audit) IS permitted and REQUIRED iff the loop terminates on a DETERMINISTIC gate — `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` exit 0 and the runtime-demo real-check, never an LLM read of "looks clean" — AND a SEPARATE fresh adversarial audit (not the implementer) reports every acceptance criterion CONFIRMED. `done ≡ (deterministic gate exit 0) AND (every success criterion CONFIRMED)` — even if the audit's total findings are nonzero. **Audit posture:** the audit brief carries the task's filed `acceptance_criteria` verbatim; the auditor reports each criterion by id as CONFIRMED or a finding, never grades severity, never recommends; the orchestrator computes GO/NO-GO per criterion (any criterion false → NO-GO), and the per-criterion verdicts are filed as the verification item the turn the audit returns. Inside the loop the orchestrator ends each turn by dispatching the next agent (fix → re-audit → fix …) — its message terminates in the next tool call, never in a report that yields control — and surfaces to the user ONLY at a genuine LLM-judgement fork (a scope/value/authority decision, e.g. a HELD merge or release per the release-hold). A criterion-falsifying finding from the audit/probe is work the loop closes autonomously, scoped to the finding's whole class, not an issue handed to the user; handing a policy-determined criterion-falsifying fix to the user instead of looping it is the mandate-008 misread this exception forecloses. A finding that falsifies no criterion is FILED and not fixed in this task; trivia (cosmetic/style, language WARNs, pre-existing/unrelated/flaky) is non-blocking and recorded as a Flag.

evidence.quote: It should be clear that “scope observations” are meaningless and non-thing in this project. We are literally cleaning all up the deferred and abandoned yet raised known work.

### W2 — four investigated gap candidates, verdict "file-new", never filed; payloads in ephemeral tmp
- **Evidence:** agent return 2026-07-13T09:30:17Z: "Complete. Nothing was filed; payloads only. **Report:** `analysis/2026-07-13-wave3-residual-filings.md`". The committed report (HEAD `2624d2c7`) verdicts all four **file-new**: (1) dryRun preview-optimism residual set (3 instances; FGAP-148's own description anticipates them "tracked as their own filing" — a filing that does not yet exist); (2) op-layer silent unknown-param acceptance ("`dryrun` for `dryRun` on any mutation op runs the LIVE write the caller meant to preview"); (3) glue-guard shell-grammar under-block R1+R2; (4) prompt-field spec-dialect divergence (inline-task specs hard-throw as workflow steps; object `inline` silently dropped by both parsers). User frame for the disposition program, 2026-07-12T11:14:00Z: "We are literally cleaning all up the deferred and abandoned yet raised known work." The user's 2026-07-13T09:32:17Z "That's an insane mismanagement of judgment and cost." addressed the spend, not the verdicts; no later statement retires the filings.
- **Current state:** fresh sweeps (`filter-block-items --block framework-gaps --field title --op matches` on `preview|dry-run|dryRun` and `param|prompt|dialect|glue|boundary|unknown`) find no covering item; highest gap id is FGAP-152 (the status-flip gap, different content). The four payload JSONs exist ONLY at `/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-david-Projects-workflowsPiExtension/d3030496-e4e1-4bfa-8df1-1df86bac518a/scratchpad/filings/` — volatile tmp, lost on reboot/cleanup.
- **Verdict:** still-open (evaporation risk: the committed analysis preserves the substance, but the ready payloads are ephemeral).
- **Dispatch:** file the four gaps via `append-block-item` with birth `--relations` per the payloads (report §1–4 names the relate-to sets: FGAP-066/148 lineage; FGAP-144 context; FGAP-120/151/147/149/089 family; FGAP-129 class), under the user's filing grant; `context-validate` after. preview can over-report","status":"closed","priority":"P2","package":"pi-context","description":"Under --dryRun, updateContext's catalog-ahead arm short-circuits BEFORE resyncSchema (no write) and reports the schema under `resynced` as the would-act set, because the concrete resynced/migrated/blocked outcome is unknowable without running the forward migration. So a version-bump schema that a live run would `migrate` (items forward-migrated) — or `block` (no safe chain, or migrated items fail revalidation) — is previewed as `resynced`. The would-register migration declarations (migrationsRegistered) are computed accurately read-only, but a live blocked run rolls those declarations back, so --dryRun can also list migrationsRegistered a live run would not keep. This undercuts FEAT-006's stated guarantee that --dry-run reports the full set of changes update would make: the per-schema OUTCOME under dryRun is optimistic, not exact, precisely when a schema is at risk of blocking.","evidence":[{"file":"packages/pi-context/src/index.ts","reference":"updateContext catalog-ahead dryRun branch pushes name onto result.resynced (the would-act set) and computes migrationsRegistered read-only;
